sentences.text: we'll be sharing our screen here, so please let me know when the time to do so.
Yeah.
Morning.
Hey, morning, Courtney, how are you?
Good.
How are you?
I am well.
I'm well.
How was your holiday break or holiday period rather?
Yeah, it was good.
How was yours?
Not bad, not bad, a little busy, um, you know, traveling around, seeing everybody, getting the FaceTime in.
Totally, yeah, Finding time to enjoy in the meantime too.
Mhm.
Looks like Tony, hey Tony, can you hear us all
right?
I can, yeah, how's it going guys?
Not bad, not bad.
Hope you also had a a restful holiday period there.
Yeah,
it was good.
I didn't do much, but that's a good thing.
Yeah, I definitely agree.
I'm looking forward maybe to uh to one of those next year, just staying put.
Yeah.
Hey, Chris, can you hear us all right?
Yep, how are you doing?
Excellent.
Doing well, doing well.
Uh, wanted to make sure that we had that, you know, quick moment to catch up here, especially starting the year.
Um, we have a, a renewal coming up, for instance, and a bunch of new features coming into the tool.
So the key things for me today was just to kind of share, uh, maybe some of those ways to catch those new changes as they come.
Uh, we've launched, for instance, a, uh, channel that you could subscribe to which will constantly update you with the weekly updates, so we're hoping that'll be an improvement.
Uh, and then wanted to touch base on a plan to maybe get everybody trained, uh, in the tool.

We have a few more training resources, uh, ready for 2025, uh, just to make sure that everybody is is up to date with, with, you know, what's coming into the tool and can start planning around those new improvements.
Anything on your end to add to that agenda before we kind of dive in?
Not for me personally.
Yeah,
not for me either.
OK.
All right.
So jumping in, would love to maybe throw the floor your way.
Any new initiatives for 2025, any, you know, new goals, or are we looking at acquisitions in a, in a larger way this year or expanding the portfolio in any way?
Um, talk to me if there's any change there or or what you're looking forward to this year.
Um, I think we're looking.
Potentially first to some new acquisitions this year, um but nothing immediate that we can share.
So, um, Tony, Chris, anything else to add?
Um,

as far as like implementations and initiatives, I think this is going to be Uh, taper back compared to the past few years, um, as we're going through, not so much growing pains but discovering that we may have overloaded ourselves over the last few years, we're wanting to kind of take a bit of a breather and slow down with some of the implementations, um.
So it it should be a little bit lighter for us this year.
Sure, more predictable perhaps and kind of um finding the footing, is that the idea?
Got it.
Great, that's helpful for me, just context, uh, for, for how 2025 looks.

I think doing a quick retroactive look on how we've been working together, uh, you know, through some things here that I've picked up from our conversations, uh, since last, uh, last March, you know, just looking for those time savings, those operational efficiencies, um, having the benefit of everybody kind of speaking from the same information, um, any of those business goals feeling particularly Um, achieved, perhaps, I, I mean, do we feel like the teams are, are, are finding more time, are things more efficient, um, how is kind of the review of apartment IQ over that first year or so?
I mean, I definitely think it it's a lot of us to be, you know, more efficient.
There's definitely time savings that goes into this, so I would say that that that's accurate, yeah.
And I think usage in general um has ramped up as we're we've switched from Yield Star over to Reba for our pricing software um and we're kind of reevaluating.
The usage of that and just making sure that all the site teams are utilizing it correctly.

With that we're kind of evaluating the training to go back and potentially retrain and then reestablish standards really for what the site teams need to be looking at within that platform and then also within apartment IQ.

And also kind of teaching them what they should be considering, so they're not just opening the page and saying that, you know, they're checking the box to say that they look at it, but understanding what they're looking at.
What are some key points that you'd like to see the teams taking from apartment IQ for instance, versus what they're using uh Reba for and and how they're leveraging the tools?
Um,

more or less, I mean we've got a couple different scenarios going with different markets, um, you know, Hillsborough market is deeply saturated with Intel, and they're having a lot of issues with that with, um, Intel's just overall layoffs happening.
So being able to really evaluate what level.

Of occupancy and exposure our comps are are at compared to us, what level of concessions are being offered in within the market, um, two of our properties are highly affected from uh those move outs that have come, um, but from what we've seen, you know, their exposure and their occupancy is not in line with the submarket, so trying to pinpoint what's causing that.

Really when it comes down to it, those two sites are the closest properties to the Intel campus, you know, so that that that's the direct correlation really where some of the other properties, yeah, they have some uh some intel uh demographic, but they're not at the same degree that we were, um, but you know, being able to show the the teams.
What's going on with the within the submarket, uh, and making sure that we're not priced too high, but also not going way too low to the point where it just starts an endless cycle of, you know.
No, renewal trade out growth, uh, and then, you know, residents giving notice and then our rent roll starts flowing backwards.
Right, right, um, and remind me the portfolios mainly stabilized properties in those kinds of areas or do we have?
OK, got it.
Very cool.
So it's probably a little bit easier to understand um the macro trend and how it's affecting those markets.
Um, any other markets of concern for you guys?
I'd love to be able to kind of see what the usage looks like over the next couple of months in those specific markets and just make sure that they're getting the access that you want to see.
Mm.
Not that I can think of.
And
trying to pinpoint, I don't know.
Yeah.
Yeah, I know.
I mean, SoCal, the market has kind of dropped a bit more than expected, but other than that, there's not really any other markets I can think of.

One thing we, we have noticed is, um, especially with with some of our stabilized sites that they haven't seen any new development pipeline for a considerable amount of time, so you know they've been operating in the standpoint of they are essentially the top of the market and then now all of a sudden um
New lease ups that are hitting the market that you know we weren't entirely aware of that now are changing that dynamic for us.
um cross points one, venues one, kind of the Denver market in general is impacted to that degree, um.
As far as like if there were some sort of enhancement is, you know, maybe some sort of alert that could go on, not an alert, but um suggestions on, you know, potential new comps to be added um.
It's very funny that you asked that you mentioned that, uh, because just this morning we saw that uh in the next couple of days we're going to have the new supply added to the explore page.
OK, so there will be a report coming in, um, that's going to allow you to kind of see.
What the new supply is for that area.
Um.
And of course we're using government sources, we're using publicly available sources, public record sources to provide that data.
OK, fantastic.
Yeah, I think that'll help.
Excellent, glad to hear that, always glad to hear that we're building stuff that that aligns, um.
But I think that makes a lot of sense.
Let me know how I can help your team align on training as well.
Uh, I know some folks like to keep it in-house.
Uh, that said, we're happy to, to be a bigger partner with respect to training teams.
Uh, we have a 2 to 3 webinar trainings a week that are offered kind of on a regular schedule.
We're also happy to do a custom schedule of training if you want to perhaps do a 4 week series where we have set times throughout the week.

Uh, that's been helpful to a lot of our partners, um, to, to get the word out and and make sure that the class is focused around sequoia and perhaps focused around the sequoia workflows and and the things you want folks to be taking from IQ.
Yeah.
Just touching on the success planning idea, I think it really kind of echoes what we talk about here in our business goals, but just, you know, are there operational efficiencies?
Are we getting over the delays with concessions, for instance, that we saw last year?
Are are the marketing teams using the tool?
Are the regionals getting what they need out of the tool?
And are those underperforming properties having the usage that we wanted to see?

Um, sounds like for the most part we're working, working towards that, um, but definitely let me know if you're seeing a big delta here for for thinking that apartment IQ is missing the mark in any way.
No, I mean, I would love to see community managers go into it more.

Yeah, because we still have some managers that, you know, shun change and whatnot, and they're still using our our old comp survey Excel spreadsheets, um, but I think most of the regionals go in there a lot now and get the data and and everything,
so it's good.
Excellent.
I think something I can do is maybe reach out to those folks in the community manager space as long as they've logged into apartment IQ once.
I do have a record of whether they're a community manager versus a regional, and I can start, uh, just kind of saying, hey, this is what you get out of apartment IQ.
I can send them update emails, for instance, uh, not to quite bombard their inboxes, but to just remind them of the tool and and how easy it is to log in.
I think we're OK.
We have talked to them and we ping them on it and stuff like that, and we'd rather kind of go through our internal flow, um, to try to get them, yeah, going.
I mean it's slowly happening.
They are converting on everything, especially when the animals are like, hey, look at your, you know, this is your concept in from IQ and and go into it.
So it is slowly happening, changing, you know, it's just kind of a slow process.
Sure, fair enough.
All right.
Um, so to that point, the usage looks good.
I do see folks from all sorts of, uh, self-described camps.
Uh, I see that the reports are being scheduled, for instance, those PDFs, even the Excel reports are being scheduled, uh, so, so that's really good news.

One thing I wanted to talk about was potentially the market surveys for research, um, that ties into the explore page a little bit, uh, but does this kind of idea of folks using scheduled reports, do we want folks using scheduled reports, for instance, or do we prefer that they're logging in?
Cause I can read this kind of two ways.
One, that's great that they're using this feature, uh, 2, does it really align with with how you'd like to see folks logging into the tool.
I'm fine with it, um.
I don't see it as, you know, a terrible thing either way.
As long as they have the data I just reading it.
Yeah, exactly, right, right.
Um, but this explore page, for instance, uh, when I come in, right, have we used this very much yet at all?
I'll just open up Denver for instance to talk a little bit about
it.
I have not, Phil.
I
personally have not used it much, but I need to.
Yeah, I was gonna say I I'd like to learn a little bit more.
I think we covered it really briefly.
Yeah, it was really brief and we do have a bunch of uh improvements.
The new supply was just added this past week.
Um, so this is gonna be a new feature and then we'll have demographics as well added here.
Um, so just to give us an idea, we're focused on the Denver metro area.
Our first tab highlighted that 30 day market snapshot for rental performances, so a lot of the same stuff you would find in your market surveys.
That historical data that you would find in the market surveys lives here as well.
That exposure that we're comparing to, now you'll you'll have an exposure measure for this zip code as a whole, that you can start comparing your properties to.
But then we'll have new supply.
You'll see the
Way we are tracking that there.
This way it doesn't get all cluttered in with the other pieces, it's just a different overlay here.
You'll find
Deliveries by quarter versus the unit distribution by property size.
And see how the unit mixes will ultimately work out when we have demographics.
So if we're seeing a lot of, for instance, growth in the in the families, we'll want to maybe build fewer one bedrooms, uh, and vice versa.

And then the bottom right hand corner as you engage with the map, you'll find total value, so existing units, 163,000 that we're picking up here in this section of Denver, but we have 3,978 under construction currently, another 700 scheduled.
And pending approvals for 15,000 units more.
We also have filters here to help you hone in on this data a little bit more.
So rent and performance on every unit that we can find versus the ability to highlight for a specific year built or renovation date.
You mentioned that a lot of your units are at the top of the market, maybe there's some of the newer ones.
Um, I would go in there, for instance, and set a floor of 2020.
Uh, and then perhaps a unit.
A unit mix or a construction type.

A lot of folks are moving into the BTR space or the SFR space, this units type and and stores construction type has been a really helpful tool for them to isolate perhaps those garden style apartments or those garden style buildings in that space.
And then once you find a Property that you're interested in?
The research tool allows you to create an on-demand market survey for that particular property.

So if we were looking to expand into other areas, we could use that research tool to our benefit there and maybe explore a new neighborhood and see what comps are like and see what pricing is like in the same way that you would for an owned property in the market survey.

Really a tool that that helps the, you know, underwriting teams, investment teams, um, but potentially also for you guys to just better understand different neighborhoods or submarkets within the Denver market alone.
Hope that sounds interesting.
Yeah, it totally does.
Uh, usage as a whole looks really solid.
Uh, the way I read this, we have our dark purple line that shows how many monthly active users there are.
We're pretty much hovering between 85 and 100 users a month.
Uh, the weekly active users, I think bears a little bit more weight because these are folks that are power users for the most part, they're logging in at least once a week.
And so their pattern definitely reflects a lull in the Christmas time period, the holiday period, uh, but it seems like we're picking up, uh, and everybody's getting in the tool.
This orange graph is daily users.
It always has this kind of high and low that corresponds with the beginning of the week, since that's when most folks are logging in and getting their um Mondays started.
So I would say this looks pretty good to me.
Uh, we have about 59, I think we have exactly 59 market surveys in your apartment IQ instance.
I always want to see somewhere between 30 and 50% of the users logging in or a factor of 30 to 50%.
So, um, if I've got 59, 60 market surveys, I'd want to see somewhere between 100 and 75 users every month, uh, because that just tells me a little bit about.
Uh, each market survey having maybe 2 users associated with them, and each of them are logging in at least once a month.
So that's kind of a baseline, but we can of course revisit, especially as you guys analyze what you want usage to look like, we can of course start revisiting this with more detail too.
But how does that picture sound?
How's that picture look?
Does it align with what you expect so far?
Yeah, totally.
Yeah, makes sense.
Great, great, great, great.
New features, fun stuff.
Um, the last thing I have a slide for would be these unit style filters.

We talked a lot about new features back in September when we last connected, I think it was, um, uh, in this style of a meeting here, uh, a lot of the unit style has come in and, and really been helpful to isolate classic units against renovated units or to pull out penthouses, um, which, which really helps, I think, find the nuance in your, in your reporting.

We've also been able to launch this change log, and that was kind of what I was talking about at the start of our call here is just the ability to kind of see how Apartment IQ is moving, what the improvements are.

Uh, we have all these great tags that will help define whether this is a brand new feature that we've added or it's something that we've approved upon or iterated upon, uh, just to use October 1 as kind of a benchmark.
Uh, we've added 14 new features since then, uh, 5 have been, uh, improved features.
So, I've dropped the link here.
I'll share this with you all in our email after the call.
Uh, you can subscribe to that so that you're always up to date on the change log.
And the way it looks here.
You can subscribe there, even works with RSS feeds, uh, but we've got some neat filters too, that's gonna allow you to see what is new, what's improved, for instance.
And what is something that we fixed if there was a bug or an issue?
So we'll also have labels that tie it to the specific part of the product.
So the research report, which we talked about, allowing you to have that market survey comp set for an unowned property.
The market survey functionality that you're already working with and comfortable with, and then things that that improved the explore functionality that we just demoed.
So a great way to just kind of see how we're delivering for you and the team.
Here we're kind of describing an improvement that allowed us to draw a specific area in the explore page, for instance, that was a big one.
But if I just hit new here.
Uh, here you'll find that literally yesterday, we added the new supply data.
Oftentimes it's a little um move, it's a GIF or GIF that'll describe exactly how to access these tools, but now we're doing time ranges in the market survey so that you can um kind of see that.
Trend, and we're moving towards a place where you'll be able to say date X and Y, and then replicate a market survey for that kind of date.
That's where we're moving towards.
Oftentimes we'll have help center articles linked as well for any additional instruction or direction to these tools.
Um

Filters on the edit comps page, for instance, that was a big one, that was a big ask, uh, so that we could make sure that we're surfacing more relevant comps rather than just showing every single comp, uh, in a market.
We added the lease units by week in the historical area, which has really allowed folks to to get a better understanding of what velocity looks like.
Um, is this something maybe that is particularly helpful for you guys as you're trying to benchmark in some of those competitive areas?
Um, a little bit.
I know.
We do kind of take like the exposure, occupancy, all that with a grain of salt just because there's the potential for units to be hidden.
Um, but with the leasing, I don't think we've really focused too much on this with our teams.
Got it.

I know a lot of folks are looking to try and and move towards leasing as a as more of a benchmark just because of that grain of salt uh factor with what's advertised and what may not be advertised, um.
But I guess it all depends on on how many units you think are often hidden in your markets.
Uh, do you guys feel like that happens a lot, uh, at your properties?
Yeah, I do.
Um, also for the, I had a question for this lease in it, so like when someone leases and like cancels or get denied.
Like, how does it show that at all or like it does it just go back on, you know, add that unit back on later.
Great question, Tony.
Yeah, we did actually add something helpful here.
I'm gonna go ahead and hit select all units, and you'll find in the property summary.
We have an application status.
This is a new status we launched, I want to say maybe sometime last quarter.
And what applications is going to do when a unit leaves the website, it's going to sit in this application's status.
It's gonna sit here for 7 days.
We did a few audits with some of our big partners that uh we work with now over half of the top 10 NMHC partners in the country.
So we did audits for 3 of their books and found that on average, uh, if a unit gets applied to, it converts within 7 days.
If it, so as long as it stays off the website for more than 7 days, we became very, very confident within a 98, 99% confidence interval that it would become a lease.
So that's why applications over 7 days, by the 8th day, a unit moves into the lease's status.
And then it will stay there.
Unless we find it surfaced again.
On websites.
In which case it will go to this relisted unit status here.
So I can click that and see what's happened to these units that were relisted.
Are they still available or have they since been applied to again?
So if this was re-listed in the last 7 days.
Did somebody apply to it?
Or is it still available?
And so in that way we're hoping to track a little bit better and and remove some of those units that just fail to convert for whatever reason.

Uh, even if they come off a website maybe for a couple of days for make ready perhaps for a renovation, or repainting this week, so we took it off the website for a few days, uh, this way it doesn't muddle the lease's data as much.
Does that make sense?
and does that kind of help a little bit or or do you feel like, yeah,
great.
We also changed just to kind of continue talking about some of our logic, we changed how we calculate a vacant unit.
It used to be that if the property had a unit that was available today.
It was vacant.
But now we account for a 10 day make ready.
Uh, I know a lot of folks aim for 7, but it more often ends up looking closer to 10.

Uh, I don't know what you guys use for make ready, uh, but we decide that if apartment has a unit available today or within the next 10 days, we'll count it as vacant, uh, to provide a more accurate occupancy value.
We did an audit with Willow Bridge specifically actually and found that we came within 99% accuracy on their advertised occupancies, um, and that was across their lease of properties too.

So really exciting stuff there, just getting closer and closer, uh, as long as there's folks participating in in the withholding of units, it's always gonna be with a grain of salt, but we are getting a lot closer every every month that we audit our data.
Cool, so keeping track of time and kind of where we are today.
Uh, this is what our portfolio looks like, 59 properties, 1400 units, uh, our renewal dates coming up here in January, February, March.
Uh, we're looking at a 15% flat increase because we believe that the product improved by that much, but I know I'll speak to Justin a little bit about that.
Um, but that's where we are right now.
Love to know if you guys are like giving the thumbs up for renewal or if we think we need to look at something else, um, but would love to get the temperature of this camp.
Um, I, I mean we love the product and everything like that.
It's just about how our budgets look, um, as they're still getting final, you know, final approval and everything like that, and I think Justin can speak more to that going forward.
Yeah.
Absolutely.

As long as you guys aren't screaming like, hey, this tool is not doing the job for me, um, because that I would love to know, so that I can make sure to address that and and make sure we get that in our products uh feedback.
Um,
Yeah, I know,
I mean we've had great feedback so far and everything and, you know, and Courtney and Chris, do you have any other, you know, feedback?
No, I mean, I've personally really enjoyed using the product.

I, I think the thing I appreciate most is how you guys are always looking to change it and honestly how quick you make changes and um yeah, just how how strategic you guys are to get those, you know, changes in place and so I know I think I can speak for all of us when I say we really appreciate you guys for that.
Thanks for saying that.
Yeah, you know, whenever
we run into issues, the support team is really quick to get them corrected, um, you know, and it's, it's no one's fault.
A lot of the, the changes that we've seen have been directly related to the property changing ownership or, you know, changing to a new website which has complete structure.
Right, and I'll definitely let them know because I know, uh, being on that support side can be, can be a tough uh gig, so I'll let them know.
Thank you, thanks so much for the kind words there, guys.
um.
I appreciate the time as always.
Uh, like I said, we can keep these monthly meetings super light.
If it doesn't work for everybody, feel free to decline next month or the month after, uh, but just want to make sure we have this channel open in case anything's coming up.
I'll reach out to Justin to start talking about the renewal a little bit, uh, and you guys have a great rest of the rest of January.
Good luck starting the year positively.
I know it's a busy time.
Thanks, Phil.
You too.
Thank you.
You're very welcome.
Take care, everybody.
Have a good one.
Bye bye now.

conversation.title: Sequoia Equities <> Apt IQ Check in - Monthly
